name: Q8 Selective Projection Quantization
hypothesis: 8-bit selective quantization on attention/MLP projection matrices can keep perplexity almost unchanged while reducing effective storage.
implementationSpec: Apply per-channel 8-bit weight quantization to self-attention and MLP projection layers, keep embeddings and normalization in high precision.
risks: compression gain may be lower than aggressive low-bit schemes

name: Q6 Balanced Group Quantization
hypothesis: 6-bit group-wise quantization can improve compression beyond 8-bit while keeping perplexity within a small acceptable drift.
implementationSpec: Apply grouped 6-bit quantization with round-wise group size refinement to attention and MLP projections.
risks: quality can regress if grouping is too coarse

name: Q4 Aggressive Quantization
hypothesis: 4-bit aggressive quantization can maximize compression, but may significantly affect perplexity.
implementationSpec: Apply 4-bit group-wise quantization to major projection layers and selected embedding/head matrices for maximal size reduction.
